title: Multimodal neurological monitoring system
abstract: Presented herein are systems and techniques for using an external component/device to collect eye movement data that is complementary to neurological measures/measurements (e.g., EEG measurements, inner ear measurements, or other electrophysiological measurements) obtained by an implantable component/device. Recipient eye movement can be tracked directly (e.g., with smart glasses) and/or indirectly (e.g., using eye movement-related eardrum oscillations (EMREO) measurements). The systems and techniques presented herein can use an implantable component (e.g., a cochlear implant, an EEG implant, etc.) to obtain electrophysiological measurements from a recipient. According to aspects presented herein, the electrophysiological measurements and the eye movement measurements are analyzed together in order to, for example, diagnosis a medical condition, predict or characterize a medical event, etc.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising : obtaining a set of electrophysiological measurements captured by an implantable device, wherein the electrophysiological measurements originate from a recipient of the implantable device; obtaining measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient by an external device, wherein the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient are synchronized with the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable device; and at least one of diagnosing a medical condition or predicting or characterizing a medical event based on an analysis that combines the electrophysiological measurements originating from the recipient and the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: associating the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient by the implantable device with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising: storing the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient in association with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient in a memory. 4. The method of claim 1, 2, or 3, wherein the set of electrophysiological measurements comprises a plurality of electroencephalography (EEG) measurements or a plurality of inner ear measurements. 5. The method of claim 1, 2, or 3, wherein obtaining the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient comprises: directly tracking the movement of the eyes of the recipient using a wearable device. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the wearable device comprises glasses configured to be worn on the face of the recipient to directly track the movement of the eyes of the recipient. 7. The method of claim 1, 2, or 3, wherein obtaining the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient comprises: indirectly detecting the movement of the eyes of the recipient based on eye movement-related eardrum oscillations (EMREO) using a sound processing unit or an ear worn device. 8. A system comprising: an implantable component configured to obtain electrophysiological measurements from a recipient of the implantable component; an external component configured to obtain measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient; and at least one processor configured to associate the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient by the implantable component with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient obtained by the external component. 9. The system of claim 8, further comprising: at least one memory configured to store the electrophysiological measurements in association with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient. 10. The system of claim 8, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: synchronize the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient with the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable component. 11. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: diagnose a medical condition based on an analysis that combines the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient and the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient. 12. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the at least one processor is configured to: 

 at least one of predict or characterize a medical event based on an analysis that combines the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient and the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient. 13. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the external component is a wearable device including a sensor that is configured to directly track movement of the eyes of the recipient. 14. The system of claim 13, wherein the wearable device comprises glasses configured to be worn on the face of the recipient. 15. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the external component is a sound processing unit or an ear worn device including a sensor that is configured to indirectly detect movement of the eyes of the recipient based on eye movement-related eardrum oscillations (EMREO). 16. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the implantable component is an electroencephalography (EEG) implant, and the electrophysiological measurements comprise EEG measurements. 17. The system of claim 8, 9, or 10, wherein the implantable component is a cochlear implant, and the electrophysiological measurements comprise inner ear measurements. 18. The system of claim 9, wherein the at least one processor and the at least one memory are included in the external component. 19. The system of claim 9, wherein the at least one processor and the at least one memory are included in an external computing device that wirelessly communicates with the external component. 20. A system comprising: an implantable device including a wireless transmitter and an inner ear electrode array, wherein the implantable device is configured to obtain electrophysiological measurements from a recipient of the implantable device via the inner ear electrode array, and transmit the electrophysiological measurements via the wireless transmitter; and 

 an external device including a wireless receiver and a memory module, wherein the external device is configured to receive the electrophysiological measurements from the implantable device via the wireless receiver, obtain measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient that are synchronized with the electrophysiological measurements, and store the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient that are synchronized with the electrophysiological measurements in the memory module. 21. The system of claim 20, wherein the implantable device includes a stimulator unit connected with the inner ear electrode array. 22. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein the external device is further configured to synchronize the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient with the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient by the implantable device. 23. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein the external device is further configured to analyze the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient in combination with the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient to diagnose a medical condition. 24. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein the external device is further configured to: analyze the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient in combination with the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient to predict or characterize a medical event. 25. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein the implantable device is a cochlear implant, and the electrophysiological measurements comprise inner ear measurements. 26. The system of claim 19 or 20, further comprising: an external computing device including a wireless transceiver and a processor, wherein the external computing device is configured to: receive the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient that are synchronized with the electrophysiological measurements from the external device via the wireless transceiver; and 

 analyze the electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient in combination with the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient to diagnose a medical condition or predict or characterize a medical event. 27. The system of claim 19 or 20, wherein the measurements representative of the movement of the eyes of the recipient are obtained based on detecting eye movement-related eardrum oscillations (EMREO). 28. One or more non-transitory computer readable storage media comprising instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: obtain a set of electrophysiological measurements from a recipient of an implantable device; obtain measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient by an external device; and analyze the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient in association with the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable device. 29. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, wherein the instructions to analyze the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient in association with the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable device comprise instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: generate an output representative of a medical condition associated with the recipient. 30. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, wherein the instructions to analyze the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient in association with the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable device comprise instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: at least one of predict or characterize a medical event associated with the recipient. 31. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, 29, or 30, further comprising instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: synchronize the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient with the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the implantable device. 32. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, 29, or 30, further comprising instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: associate the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient by the implantable device with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient; and store the set of electrophysiological measurements obtained from the recipient in association with the measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient in a memory. 33. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, 29, or 30, wherein the instructions to obtain measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient comprises instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: obtain measurements representing a direct tracking the movement of the eyes of the recipient using a wearable device. 34. The one or more non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 28, 29, or 30, wherein the instructions to obtain measurements representative of movement of the eyes of the recipient comprises instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to: obtain measurements representing an indirect tracking of the movement of the eyes of the recipient based on eye movement-related eardrum oscillations (EMREO) using a sound processing unit or an ear worn device.
